meanings.usage: 用于描述值得称赞、非常美好或令人印象深刻的事物，可修饰未来、成就、风景等名词
口语和书面语均常用，可表示对事物的赞赏，也可描述辉煌的经历、建筑等
非正式用法，通常用单数形式，the rub 指某个情况中最难解决的问题所在
表示用某物在表面来回移动擦拭的动作
指一个人区别于他人的固有特征，也可表示对自身所属群体的归属感

meanings.meaning: 沉闷的；枯燥乏味的；令人沮丧的
meanings.usage: 用于描述事物或氛围无聊、缺乏趣味，使人感到消沉
后常接介词to，表示对某事物的投入、承诺或责任
用于描述人在没有得到许可的情况下做超出自己权限的事，带有贬义
用于形容人或行为缺乏理智、十分荒唐
指被过度使用以至于失去原有新意的表达或观点，带有贬义
可描述和宇宙相关的事物，也可引申表示极其宏大、不受世俗局限的
除了表示地理位置在郊区，也可引申形容人思想偏狭、平淡无趣，带有贬义色彩
用于描述人过于高估自己，看不起他人，含贬义
常用复数形式fundamentals，指某一领域最核心、最基础的规则或事实
用于形容事物是核心的、至关重要的
后常接介词to，表示对某人、某事物的热爱或投入

phrases.usage: 正式用语，用于礼貌地表示自己要做可能稍显冒昧的事，可译为“冒昧做某事，擅自做某事”

examples.en: If there is one -- which I take leave to doubt -- then it is older people who create it, not the young themselves.
examples.zh: 如果确有这样一个问题的话——恕我对此表示怀疑——那也是老年人而非年轻人自己造成的。

examples.en: I take leave to disagree with your view on this project.
examples.zh: 请恕我冒昧，我不赞同你对这个项目的看法。

phrases.usage: 表示开始探讨最核心、最根本的问题，get down to 意为“开始认真处理，着手做”

examples.en: Let us get down to fundamentals and agree that the young are after all human beings -- people just like their elders.
examples.zh: 让我们来认真探讨一下根本问题，先承认年轻人毕竟也是人——和他们的长辈一样的人。

examples.en: Instead of arguing about trivial details, we should get down to fundamentals of the plan.
examples.zh: 我们与其争论无关紧要的细节，不如好好探讨这个计划的根本问题。

phrases.usage: 常用于提醒对方某个容易被忽略的重要事实或理由，可译为“毕竟，终究”

examples.en: Let us get down to fundamentals and agree that the young are after all human beings -- people just like their elders.
examples.zh: 让我们来认真探讨一下根本问题，先承认年轻人毕竟也是人——和他们的长辈一样的人。

examples.en: You shouldn't scold him too much; after all, he is only a child.
examples.zh: 你别太责怪他了，毕竟他还只是个孩子。

phrases.usage: 表示忙于做某事、参与某事，是正式表达

examples.en: For one thing, being a problem gives you a certain identity, and that is one of the things the young are busily engaged in seeking.
examples.zh: 首先，成为问题能给你一种特定的身份，而这正是年轻人忙着追求的东西之一。

examples.en: The researchers are engaged in studying the effect of climate change on local ecosystems.
examples.zh: 研究人员正忙于研究气候变化对当地生态系统的影响。

phrases.usage: 用于描述人或事物带有某种气质、氛围，air在此处意为“神态，气质，氛围”

examples.en: They have an air of freedom, and they have not a dreary commitment to mean ambitions or love of comfort.
examples.zh: 他们有一种自由的气质，不会乏味地投身于卑俗的野心或对安逸的追求中。

examples.en: There is an air of mystery around the old castle at the top of the hill.
examples.zh: 山顶上的那座古堡周围有一种神秘的氛围。

phrases.usage: 指试图通过结交上流社会人士来提高自身社会地位的人，含贬义，可译为“趋炎附势的人，想方设法跻身上流社会的人”

examples.en: They are not anxious social climbers, and they have no devotion to material things.
examples.zh: 他们不是急着往上爬的趋炎附势之徒，也不执着于物质事物。

examples.en: Everyone could tell that she was a social climber who only made friends with rich people.
examples.zh: 所有人都能看出来她是个趋炎附势的人，只和有钱人交朋友。

phrases.usage: 表示“与……形成对比，和……相比”，用于突出两者之间的差异

examples.en: It's as if they were in some sense cosmic beings in violent and lovely contrast with us suburban creatures.
examples.zh: 从某种意义上说，他们仿佛是宇宙的子民，与我们这些世俗庸人形成了强烈而美好的对比。

examples.en: In contrast with last year's cold winter, this winter is unusually warm.
examples.zh: 和去年寒冷的冬天相比，今年的冬天异常暖和。

phrases.usage: 表示“向……求助以获得某物”，是常用的固定搭配

examples.en: He may be conceited, ill-mannered, presumptuous or fatuous, but I do not turn for protection to dreary cliches about respect for elders -- as if mere age were a reason for respect.
examples.zh: 他可能自负、无礼、放肆、愚蠢，但我不会用“要尊重长辈”这种陈腐的套话来为自己撑腰——好像仅凭年龄就值得尊重似的。

examples.en: When you meet difficulties in study, you can turn to your teacher for help.
examples.zh: 你在学习上遇到困难时，可以向老师求助。

grammar.title: 强调句 (Emphatic Sentence)
grammar.definition: 用于突出句子中的某个成分（如主语、宾语、状语等），表达强烈语气的句式，常译为“正是……，就是……”
grammar.structure: It is/was + 被强调部分 + that/who + 句子其余部分
grammar.usage: 被强调部分指人时可用who/that，指其他事物时只能用that；即使被强调部分是复数，be动词依然用is/was

examples.en: If there is one -- which I take leave to doubt -- then it is older people who create it, not the young themselves.
examples.zh: 如果确有这样一个问题的话——恕我对此表示怀疑——那也是老年人而非年轻人自己造成的。

examples.en: It is your hard work rather than luck that leads to your success.
examples.zh: 正是你的努力而非运气造就了你的成功。

grammar.title: as if 引导的虚拟语气
grammar.definition: as if意为“好像，仿佛”，当引导的从句表示的内容与事实不符或者是假想的情况时，需要使用虚拟语气
grammar.structure: 主句 + as if + 从句（从句用相应的过去时态表示虚拟）
grammar.usage: 表示与现在事实相反时，从句动词用一般过去时（be动词常用were，所有人称通用）；表示与过去事实相反时用过去完成时

examples.en: It's as if they were in some sense cosmic beings in violent and lovely contrast with us suburban creatures.
examples.zh: 从某种意义上说，他们仿佛是宇宙的子民，与我们这些世俗庸人形成了强烈而美好的对比。

examples.en: He talks as if he knew everything about this field, but actually he is a newcomer.
examples.zh: 他说起话来好像对这个领域无所不知，但实际上他只是个新人。

grammar.title: as 引导的方式状语从句
grammar.definition: as可引导方式状语从句，表示“按照，如同，就像”，说明动作的方式或两者的相似性
grammar.usage: 用于比较两个动作或情况的相似之处，从句通常是完整的句子，口语中可省略部分成分

examples.en: I accept that we are equals, and I will argue with him, as an equal, if I think he is wrong.
examples.zh: 我认为我们是平等的，如果我觉得他错了，我会以平等的身份和他争论。

examples.en: When in Rome, do as the Romans do.
examples.zh: 入乡随俗。

sentencePatterns.pattern: People are always talking about + 名词/名词短语.

original.en: People are always talking about 'the problem of youth'.
original.zh: 人们总是在谈论“青年问题”。

imitations.en: People are always talking about the impact of social media on teenagers.
imitations.zh: 人们总是在谈论社交媒体对青少年的影响。

imitations.en: People are always talking about the best way to keep healthy in modern society.
imitations.zh: 人们总是在谈论现代社会保持健康的最佳方式。

sentencePatterns.pattern: There is only one difference between A and B: A... and B...

original.en: There is only one difference between an old man and a young one: the young man has a glorious future before him and the old one has a splendid future behind him.
original.zh: 老人和年轻人之间只有一个区别：年轻人的前面有辉煌的未来，而老人的辉煌未来已经在身后了。

imitations.en: There is only one difference between optimistic people and pessimistic people: optimistic people always look for opportunities in difficulties, and pessimistic people always see difficulties in opportunities.
imitations.zh: 乐观的人和悲观的人之间只有一个区别：乐观的人总能在困难中寻找机会，而悲观的人总能在机会中看到困难。

imitations.en: There is only one difference between regular exercise and occasional exercise: regular exercise brings long-term health benefits, and occasional exercise only provides short-term comfort.
imitations.zh: 规律运动和偶尔运动只有一个区别：规律运动能带来长期的健康益处，而偶尔运动只能提供短期的安慰。

sentencePatterns.pattern: I find + 名词 + 形容词.

original.zh: 我觉得年轻人很有活力。

imitations.en: I find reading books about history very interesting.
imitations.zh: 我觉得读历史相关的书非常有意思。

imitations.en: I find the process of learning a new language challenging but rewarding.
imitations.zh: 我觉得学习一门新语言的过程虽然有挑战，但很有收获。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Sb may be..., but I do not... -- as if...

original.en: He may be conceited, ill-mannered, presumptuous or fatuous, but I do not turn for protection to dreary cliches about respect for elders -- as if mere age were a reason for respect.
original.zh: 他可能自负、无礼、放肆、愚蠢，但我不会用“要尊重长辈”这种陈腐的套话来为自己撑腰——好像仅凭年龄就值得尊重似的。

imitations.en: He may be inexperienced and make mistakes sometimes, but I do not deny his value to the team -- as if seniority alone means everything.
imitations.zh: 他可能经验不足，偶尔会犯错，但我不会因此否定他对团队的价值——好像资历本身就代表一切似的。

imitations.en: This method may have some small flaws, but I do not reject it completely -- as if there was a perfect solution in the world.
imitations.zh: 这个方法可能有一些小缺陷，但我不会完全否定它——好像世界上存在完美的解决方案似的。
